title: Aussies making AI work for them
full_text:
Australians professionals are making artificial intelligence work harder for them than others around the world, new research from Microsoft has revealed, but the majority still fear they’re falling behind.
The Big Tech firm’s annual Work Trends Index surveyed 2000 Australian full-time and self-employed workers, and found more than two thirds are concerned about falling behind if they don’t adapt to AI quickly. Yet the majority – 51 per cent – said it still feels safer to focus on their current goals than “to redesign work” with AI. Only 13 per cent reported that they’re “rewarded for reinvention even when it doesn’t immediately produce results”.
AI expenditure has grown exponentially, however other recent surveys have indicated most Aussie businesses are yet to see a return on their investment.
The Microsoft report also found 63 per cent of Australian workers who are using AI are now producing work they couldn’t have done a year ago. The figure was significantly higher than the global average (58 per cent), and even higher for workers described as “frontier professionals”, 84 per cent of whom are now getting more out of their work day.
